nameMeanings.name: Pudsey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Pudsey (WR Yorks), which is recorded as Podechesai(e) in 1086, Pudekesseya in the 12th century, and Pudekeshay in 1219. The place-name probably derives from an Old English personal name * Pudoc (genitive * Pudoces) + Old English ēg ‘island’. Pudsey stands on the top of a hill, and so in this case ēg may have the sense ‘hill spur, island of good land in moorland’. Alternatively, the final element may be Old English (ge)hæg ‘enclosure’ or hēah ‘high ground, height’.

nameMeanings.name: Florence
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval form of the Latin masculine name Florentius (a derivative of florens ‘blossoming, flourishing’) and its feminine form Florentia. In the Middle Ages the name was commonly borne by men (as, for example, the historian Florence of Worcester), but it is now exclusively a girl's name. This was revived in the second half of the 19th century, being given in honour of Florence Nightingale ( 1820–1910 ), the founder of modern nursing, who organized a group of nurses to serve in the Crimean War. She herself received the name because she was born in the Italian city of Florence (Latin Florentia, Italian Firenze).

variants.name: Florance
meanings: Medieval form of the Latin masculine name Florentius (a derivative of florens ‘blossoming, flourishing’) and its feminine form Florentia. In the Middle Ages the name was commonly borne by men (as, for example, the historian Florence of Worcester), but it is now exclusively a girl's name. This was revived in the second half of the 19th century, being given in honour of Florence Nightingale ( 1820–1910 ), the founder of modern nursing, who organized a group of nurses to serve in the Crimean War. She herself received the name because she was born in the Italian city of Florence (Latin Florentia, Italian Firenze).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Florence Pudsey was born about 1476, in Barforth Hall, Yorkshire, England, her father, Henry Pudsey Esq., was 28 and her mother, Margaret Conyers, was 32. She married Thomas Talbot of Bashall about 1493. She died about 1558, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Florence Pudsey est née environ 1476, à Barforth Hall, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Henry Pudsey Esq., avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margaret Conyers, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Talbot of Bashall environ 1493. Elle est décédée environ 1558, à l’âge de 84 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Florence Pudsey um 1476 in Barforth Hall, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry Pudsey Esq. 28 und ihre Mutter Margaret Conyers 32. Sie hat um 1493 Thomas Talbot of Bashall geheiratet. Sie starb um 1558 im Alter von 84 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Florence Pudsey è nata il circa 1476 nel Barforth Hall, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Henry Pudsey Esq., aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Margaret Conyers, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Talbot of Bashall circa 1493. Morì circa 1558, all'età di 84 anni.
